what_it_does: This file is a handoff brief for the I4 milestone: it instructs the implementer to converge the project-initialization WizardScreen onto the same unified stream runner used by embedded guided streams, lists required reading, already-completed work, two candidate approaches, guardrails, acceptance checks, and a current file map.
observations: It frames I4 as the highest-risk change because the wizard is the front door.
It mandates a research-first reading order of ten files before coding.
It documents two viable paths (A: runner gains a synthesize step kind; B: shared engine module) and recommends A.
It lists substantial already-done work: adaptive stream engine, StreamRunner, StreamHost, init stream, fixed shapes, etc.
It includes explicit guardrails: tokens via MS_TOKENS/var(--*), TDD in run_script sandbox, big verified increments, fork_verifier_agent after done.
It provides five acceptance checks covering front door, SHAPE phase, project creation, embedded runner, and console cleanliness.
It ends with a generative system file map listing core JS/JSX/CSS/SQL files.
standouts: Highest-risk unification: the front-door wizard must become a consumer of the unified stream runner rather than a parallel implementation.
Recommended approach A is to add a synthesize step kind to the runner and run the existing init stream end-to-end inside a full-screen host.
The brief-to-answers mapping is identified as the crux: init ask-move answers must populate the brief consumed by MS_SYNTH_AI.
Guardrails emphasize TDD pure logic in the run_script sandbox and loud failure with no silent fallback.
conventions: Follows a handoff convention: one job, read-first list, already-done list, approach options with recommendation, guardrails, verification, and file map.
Uses project-specific abbreviations (MS_INTAKE, MS_SYNTH_AI, MS_STREAMS, MS_LAUNCH, MS_RESOLVE, MS_TOKENS, MS_STATUS) without defining them in the file.
Uses uppercase section headers and bold labels for scannability.
Does not contain code; references code symbols by file path and component/function name.
concerns: The file asserts the work is highest-risk but does not quantify the risk or cite prior failures beyond narrow reading is the known failure mode.
Approach A is recommended but the verification checks do not explicitly cover the new synthesize step kind or a rollback plan if the runner change destabilizes the front door.
The already done list is long and could drift from actual state by the time a reader picks up the handoff.
No explicit owner, date, or status field, making freshness hard to judge.
notes: A risk-managed handoff that wants the wizard to disappear into the existing stream runner while keeping the front door intact.
questions: What is the current divergence between WizardScreen and StreamRunner in lines of code and data flow?
How is the brief object structured and which fields are mandatory for MS_SYNTH_AI.directions()?
What does fork_verifier_agent mean procedurally and which checks must it run?
What is the exact definition of big but verified increments in terms of commit or PR boundaries?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose is to hand off the I4 unification task; the file does exactly that by providing context, reading order, options, guardrails, and verification criteria.
apparent_themes: unification of front-door and embedded flows
intention_signals: It is trying to prevent a narrow, risky refactor by forcing broad context reading, reusing the battle-tested adaptive stream engine and MS_SYNTH_AI, and keeping project creation intact while eliminating the parallel wizard implementation.
novelty: It proposes making the front-door wizard a consumer of the same runner that powers in-page sub-sequences, rather than treating setup as a special standalone flow.
contribution: It contributes a risk-managed transition plan that preserves existing adaptive engines and renderers, defines a clear brief-to-answers mapping problem, and supplies concrete acceptance checks for unifying the project-init path with the embedded stream runner.
summary_for_embedding: A handoff document describing how to converge the project-initialization WizardScreen into the unified stream runner, including required reading, recommended approach, guardrails, and acceptance checks.
intention_for_embedding: To guide an implementer through a high-risk unification of the front-door wizard with the existing adaptive stream runner while preserving project creation and verification standards.
